title: Opening Night - Doors open
description:
Doors open at 5pm, and content kicks off at Centre Stage at 6pm. You’ll need to be registered and have collected your wristband and lanyard to enter.

Speakers include Sigrid Jin, Founder Member of Sionic AI; Gregor Robertson, PacifiCan Minister at Government of Canada; Hon. Brenda Bailey, Minister of Finance of British Columbia; Ken Sim, Mayor of Vancouver; and Paddy Cosgrave, CEO of Web Summit.

Join us for an unforgettable evening filled with great speakers, networking, and more.

We can’t wait to see you there!

Please note: The exhibition floor is closed today. The Opening Ceremony is taking place at Centre Stage.

title: Can copyright survive tokenmaxxing?
description: On March 31, Sigrid Jin tasked AI agents with replicating Claude's codebase and released it to the world. Now GitHub's fastest growing repository in history, the code library has survived scrutiny and copyright violation claims – but what does it mean to build in a world where everything can be reproduced immediately? Join the person who burned through 25 billion tokens in a year as Sigrid explores the implications of AI on copyright, open source development, and creative work.

title: The sovereign AI blueprint
description: AI will only realise its promise if nations, institutions, and industries can trust and adopt it at scale. Join Minister Evan Solomon and Cohere’s Chief AI Officer Joelle Pineau for a conversation on Canada’s AI moment, as they explore the role of sovereign AI, homegrown innovation, and responsible deployment in building systems that businesses, governments, and communities can rely on, making the tools of the future accessible, practical, and beneficial for everyone.

title: The new Go-To-Market blueprint: building predictable revenue in 2026
description: In this session, we strip away the fluff and dive into the architectural shift required to scale in today’s market. We will explore how leading organizations are moving beyond siloed departments to create a unified Revenue Engine that prioritizes signal over noise and lifetime value over short-term wins.

title: Opening remarks

title: Creative tech investing: Pitching to different types of money  [Hosted by Vancouver, BC, Canada]
description: Creative tech and gaming is a $200B industry. Learn how investing in this space differs from traditional venture models and how to approach video game investment.

title: Scaling trillions of dollars of compute: Will it turn out to be the worst bet in history?
description: "Scaling compute" is AI industry jargon for spending more and more money on data centers and the chips (typically GPUs made by Nvidia) inside those data centers. So called hyperscalers like OpenAI, Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, Microsoft, xAI and Anthropic aim to spend nearly a trillion dollars on compute this year, more than a dozen times the cost of the Manhattan project. In this talk, Gary will argue that this is a bad bet, unlikely to pay off, filled with numerous downsides for society, and that it may well lead to a recession.

title: The state of venture capital
description: Venture capital in 2026 - boom, bust, or just business as usual? As market cycles shift and investor sentiment wavers, where is the money going? This panel unpacks the latest funding trends, economic realities, and what it all means for startups and VCs navigating the year ahead.

title: From AI assistants to AI coworkers: what's next?
description: The narrative surrounding AI in the workplace has evolved with dizzying speed. We have moved beyond the era of "AI as a writing assistant" and entered a far more complex, experimental phase: the rise of the autonomous agent. These systems aren't just drafting emails; they are joining teams, translating high-level business strategies into executable tasks, and managing entire project lifecycles with minimal oversight. But is this shift truly a seamless migration of human workflows to digital agents, or are we overlooking the friction points?

title: Crafting a story investors can't ignore
description: Turn your pitch into a story investors believe. Learn why most decks fail, what investors look for, and how to clearly show customer pain, proof, and a credible path to scale—without fluff.

title: Show your work: The new sustainability standard [Hosted by veritree]
description: The standard has shifted and sustainability is no longer about intent, it's about measurable impact. Explore what that means for businesses, alongside insights from a world record-breaking tree planter. Please be aware that attendance is first-come, first-served.

title: How Brands Stay Visible When AI Decides [Hosted by Profound]
description:
Unpack one of the biggest shifts in internet history: the move from links and clicks to AI-generated answers.

Scott shares why LLMs made answer engine optimization AEO inevitable with practical tactics teams are using right now. Please be aware that attendance is first-come, first-served.

title: Opening remarks
description: Opening remarks with Jay Richards

title: Context is the new competitive advantage
description: Foundation models have transformed what AI can do. Agentic platforms are changing how work gets done. But there's a layer that most enterprise AI stacks are still missing: the operational context that tells AI not just what to do, but how the business actually works. Join Alex Rinke, Co-CEO & Co-founder of Celonis, as he makes the case that process intelligence is the essential foundation for AI that delivers real outcomes – and explain why the context layer is emerging as the defining infrastructure of the Enterprise AI era.

title: From fans to fanatics in sports and gaming
description: How do you turn casual users into obsessed communities? Learn from the worlds of sports and online gaming as leaders from Seattle Sounders and Chess.com share strategies for fan engagement, loyalty, and growth. This session uncovers the tools, data, and creative tactics that keep audiences coming back.

title: Looking west: Life sciences' new global hub
description: The life sciences and biomanufacturing strategy is a geopolitical pivot designed to turn British Columbia into the world’s most attractive destination for biotech capital. Join Minister Ravi Kahlon as he breaks down how B.C. is positioning itself as the indispensable link in the global health supply chain, attracting the brightest minds and boldest investors as well as strengthening the pipeline from discovery to manufacturing.

speakers.name: Ravi Kahlon
speakers.position: Minister of Jobs and Economic Growth of British Columbia

title: When everything is signal, what's the noise?
description: AI has produced the most crowded deal flow in venture history. Every startup claims to be transformative, every round is oversubscribed, and every investor says they saw it coming. So how do the best investors hold the line? This session explores the psychology and discipline behind genuinely differentiated AI investment and what it actually costs to follow the herd, and how to separate the best from the rest.

title: CPA Intelligence and Ethics Show
description: Welcome to the CPA Intelligence and Ethics Show. For CPAS like me, this time of AI advancement brings many emotions. Excitement, skepticism, and yes, even fear. This podcast will explore the exponential growth in AI and how that will impact the jobs, ethics, and lives of those in the CPA profession. We'll unpack what that transformation means and how CPAS can respond with integrity, insight, and innovation. Join me and expert guests as we break down the latest AI trends, ethical dilemmas, and action steps for future-ready CPAS. If you're a CPA, this podcast is your roadmap to the future.

title: AI promised productivity. It delivered workslop.
description: The AI bubble hasn't popped, but it's leaking. Despite record investment, 95% of organizations see no measurable return on AI. In this panel researchers Jeff Hancock and Kate Niederhoffer discuss the reason why: Founders and CEOs have been investing in the wrong thing.

title: Selling the AI dream
description: As AI moves from a futuristic concept to a daily reality, the primary challenge for marketers is translating complex innovation into a compelling human benefit. Join this panel to explore the art of balancing technical expertise with creative simplicity to sell the AI dream while building trust with the everyday consumer.

title: How to win in AI
description: Raising big, growing fast, or building durable systems – what actually creates advantage in AI today? Join this session as leaders who took very different paths to getting ahead share what they've learned.

title: Mastering the art of the non-valley unicorn
description: For decades, the 'golden ticket' to tech success required a Bay Area zip code. Will the advent of AI usher in a new era of regional innovation, and could the next generation of unicorns emerge from outside Silicon Valley? In this session, Techstars CEO David Cohen explores the idea that while great founders and startups are everywhere, opportunity is not evenly distributed.

title: Return of the rack: Are hardware and deep tech reclaiming the VC stack?

Join this session as we take a look at how AI, hardware, and deep tech are powering the future.

In this blunt reality check, Salil Deshpande of Uncorrelated Ventures asks whether venture is still a viable asset class for LPs, or has it finally reached its end of life?

She’ll unpack the realities of today’s IPO market and how a wave of mega-cap listings could test the limits of both the public and private markets. The session will also explore the $100B+ venture secondary market and how its growth is shaping the future of VC liquidity.

title: Hyperscaling a brand
description: What does it take to scale an AI company from startup to category leader, without losing core brand identity? Metropolis Co-Founder and Chief Integration Officer Courtney Fukuda has lived that journey firsthand, spearheading rapid growth and major acquisitions. She'll share what breaks when you scale that fast and what you have to protect at all costs.

title: Identifying use cases for AI at work: We can automate... but should we?
description: The surge of generative AI has shifted the workplace conversation from "What is possible?" to "What is responsible?" While the technical capacity to automate tasks is expanding exponentially, the strategic decision to do so requires a nuanced balance between efficiency and human value. This session explores the critical framework for vetting AI integration, moving beyond the hype to identify high-impact, ethical use cases.

title: IPHatch North America: The Startup Launchpad for Deep Tech Innovation
description: IPHatch North America gives startups powerful head start, access to patents and inventions sourced directly from renowned global corporations. By connecting deep tech founders with proven IP, IPHatch eliminates years of R&D uncertainty and accelerates the path from innovation to commercialization. Backed by a robust 5-year incubation program, startups receive sustained support, mentorship, and resources to develop, refine, and scale their ventures with confidence. With the right intellectual property and long-term backing in hand from day one, startups can focus on what matters most building, scaling, and competing on a global stage.

title: Quantum partnerships: From innovation to global advantage
description: Quantum leadership is built through collaboration. This panel explores how strategic partnerships are scaling quantum innovation across dual use applications. Hear from leaders building strong ecosystems that accelerate commercialization, strengthen global competitiveness, and convert breakthroughs into solutions delivering strategic advantage for global prosperity and security.

title: Exploring the era of creator-led luxury
description: How do you scale a brand via the creator economy without diluting its prestige? With creators evolving from mere amplifiers to the primary interface for brands, join this cross-sector panel to discover strategies for transitioning from broad awareness to deep community advocacy.

title: The AI ROI reckoning
description: VCs have poured billions into the AI stack, but are we about to face the AI ROI moment of truth? As rising compute costs collide with the reality of enterprise integration and scaling costs, the pressure to deliver tangible bottom-line impact and to prove unit economics is intensifying. Are we funding the next industrial revolution, or a generation of over-priced experiments?

title: Where does the smart money land in 2026?
description: Q1 of 2026 was the largest quarter for venture investment in many years. This session explores the sectors that are attracting the most aggressive capital today. From vertical AI agents to massive infrastructure behind next-gen energy and robotics. Join us as a panel of top VCs find out who is winning the fight to quality and which ‘sure bets’ from 2025 are losing their luster.

title: TechSurge
description: The TechSurge: Deep Tech VC Podcast shares the latest insights directly from legendary Silicon Valley leaders, daring new founders, and visionary technologists, exploring topics at the intersection of emerging technology, geopolitics, and business..

title: The AI inflection: powering the next era of II from cloud to edge
description: We are at a once-in-a-generation inflection point where artificial intelligence is reshaping every layer of the technology stack - and redefining how the world computes. From hyperscale data centers to enterprise infrastructure, personal computing, and embedded systems, demand for high-performance, energy-efficient compute is accelerating at an unprecedented pace.

title: Making the case for creative data
description: Are you just using data to report on things? This session explores how to unlock data's potential as a powerful engine for creative inspiration. VML's Global Lead of Creative Data, Jason Carmel, challenges you to view data not as a rearview mirror, but as a source of limitless creative fuel for your brand.

title: Building AI companies that go the distance
description:
Clio just closed a record-setting $1B acquisition but Jack Newton didn’t slow down; he raised the bar.
That mindset is still too rare in Canada especially as AI reshapes every industry. Lucy Hargreaves is working to change that by building the policy, narrative, and community to support more ambitious companies.
In this conversation, Lucy and Jack go beyond the headline to explore what it really takes to build and scale a global company from Canada and treat every win as a starting point, not a finish line.

title: Why the infrastructure that enabled AI is now holding It back
description: LLMs have defined the current wave of AI, but they’re only one layer. This session goes deeper into the architectures required for real-world AI, combining perception, decision systems, and execution, to move from chat-based systems to AI that can operate in complex environments.

title: Investing in a fragmented world
description: Venture capital is increasingly defined by geography. From 'sovereign AI' mandates to the decoupling of global supply chains, investment strategies are becoming inseparable from geopolitical ones. How are top VCs navigating this new reality? Join us for a high-level look at how macroeconomics is reshaping how capital flows in 2026.

title: When AI Runs Code: Why Isolation Is Now a Requirement [Hosted by Cloudflare]
description: AI agents run code now, not just write it. They install packages, run scripts, call tools inside your core environment if you let them. Sandboxes are the missing piece for safe agent architecture. Please be aware that attendance is first-come, first-served.

title: Why the best modern brands are entertainment companies
description: The brands winning attention today don’t market like advertisers. They think like studios, creators and media companies. From livestreams and podcasts to original series and cultural moments, the smartest brands are building audiences before they sell products. This session explores why entertainment has become the new competitive advantage in marketing, and what separates genuinely culture-shaping brands from companies just chasing virality.

title: Owning the engine: The case for sovereign AI
description: Digital sovereignty is no longer just about where data lives; it is about who controls the AI systems powering the national economy.  Join Bell’s Mirko Bibic and Coveo’s Louis Tetu as they outline their vision for the newly launched Canadian Sovereign AI Alliance, the importance of connectivity and compute for national resilience, and how strengthening domestic supply chains and keeping our talent in Canada can help us to lead globally.

title: AI on your terms: building products people can say no to
description: In this fireside chat, Ajit Varma will explore what it actually takes to build AI systems that users can accept, reject, or ignore. From modular architectures to maintaining independence in a Chromium-dominated ecosystem, the conversation will focus on the real engineering and product decisions behind giving people meaningful choice. It’s a different approach than much of the industry is taking. The goal is simple: build a browser that works for people who want AI, people who don’t, and everyone in between, without taking that choice away from them. Because the real safeguard in AI isn’t what it does. It’s whether users can say no.

title: Can an AI pick the next unicorn?
description: Is the era of the 'gut-feeling investor' over? Has the agentic VC arrived? Join this session as we discuss how investors are using tools to source deals, conduct due diligence, and find the next generation of unicorns.

title: The Iran war and the limits of US power
description: As tensions escalate into open conflict and the prospect of the US escalation grows, we must ask a deeper question: what happens when global power meets its limits? In this conversation, Stephen Walt and Chris Hedges examine the strategic, political, and moral consequences of a prolonged confrontation with Iran. Could this become a defining moment in the transformation of the global order and the end of US hegemony?

title: How AI is rewriting the rules of brand discovery
description: As AI agents reshape how people discover products, the rules of visibility are shifting fast. But discovery isn’t happening in a vacuum – social platforms and creator ecosystems are generating the signals that influence what surfaces. This session brings together perspectives from AI-driven discovery and social intelligence to unpack how brands get found in a world where algorithms, agents, and audiences all play a role.

Meeting at The Drop, the group will walk along the waterfront out towards Stanley Park and back. This is a outdoors walking meetup. Please bring comfortable shoes and clothing. description: Everyone talks about AI agents. Most teams are still copy-pasting chatbot outputs. title: The modern growth playbook: AI, B2B, ads and security
description: Join us for a panel that cuts to the heart of how businesses are competing for attention in an era of algorithmic feeds, AI-generated content, and rapidly shifting consumer behaviour. The Modern Growth Playbook: AI, Ads and the Battle for Customers brings together growth leaders and marketing innovators to debate what's actually working, from AI-powered targeting and personalisation to the evolving role of paid media, and what the race for customers looks like when everyone has access to the same tools.

title: Two exits, many lessons
description: Most founders obsess over how to build. Few think clearly about when, or whether, to sell. Rahul Vohra has done it twice: Rapportive to LinkedIn in 2012, and Superhuman to Grammarly in 2025, forming the combined company now known as Superhuman. In this session, he’ll share the unfiltered version: the signals that told him it was time, how each deal unfolded, what a strong process actually looks like from the inside, and the lessons that only come from doing it more than once.

title: The F1 juggernaut
description: With a global audience of 827 million people in 2025, F1 is now the world’s most popular annual sporting series. Amidst a season with new regulations, new teams, and unprecedented changes, this panel will look beyond the grid to discuss the fan economy, commercial partnerships, and global expansion that are driving the sport forward.

title: Dreaming big - talking moonshots in 2026
description: Small steps won't solve the world’s biggest problems. As we face global challenges in connectivity, climate, and infrastructure, the "10% improvement" mindset is no longer enough. Join Helen Riley and Mahesh Krishnaswamy for a deep dive into the "Moonshot" philosophy.

title: Break your AI system before customers (or attackers) do [Hosted by TELUS Digital]
description: Leaked data. Invented policies. Competitor recommendations. Learn the methods attackers use to break AI systems, the failures customers find, and how red teaming helps you test, monitor, and validate. Laptops out. Let's break AI. Please be aware that attendance is first-come, first-served.

title: Most AI projects fail. Here’s why.
description: Enterprises are racing to adopt AI, but many early projects are already stalling. Tools lack context, employees don’t trust the outputs, and promising pilots never scale into real business impact. In this fireside chat, Glean Founder & CEO Arvind Jain shares what he’s seeing and hearing from Fortune 500 companies: where they are getting stuck, why traditional approaches to software and governance break down with AI, and what the organizations achieving real results are doing differently.

title: Attracting Tomorrow’s Workforce: Solving the Tech Talent Challenge
description: The global scramble for talent has reached a fever pitch. In 2026, simply offering a competitive salary is no longer enough to secure the innovators and leaders who will drive the next decade of growth. Organizations must now navigate a landscape defined by demographic shifts, rapid technological displacement, and a workforce that prioritizes purpose as much as profit.

title: Breakout Startups
description: Get ready to witness a showcase of tomorrow's industry leaders, as innovative startups pitch their disruptive ideas and cutting-edge technologies. You'll get a fast-paced, high-energy session packed with groundbreaking concepts, all designed to capture your attention and potential investment.

title: Opening remarks
description: Opening remarks with The Knowledge Society's Kai Bundy.

title: Opening remarks

title: UpScrolled vs. the feed giants
description: The social media microblogging market is overdue for a shakeup. In this talk, we explore how UpScrolled is challenging the dominance of legacy platforms by rethinking feeds, prioritizing signal over noise and giving users back control. Can a new contender redefine the future of online conversation?

title: Clean resource innovation network: Technology showcase [Hosted by Climate Innovation Zone]
description: Discover late-stage clean tech poised to advance Canada’s climate goals, driving economic and public benefits, and shaping the future of clean energy through scalable innovation.

title: Cancer, who cares? Making the diagnosis unremarkable
description: While other conditions are monitored, tracked and continuously adjusted, cancer has rarely been managed properly in real time. Now, earlier detection and more efficient treatment are stripping cancer of its status as a life-altering diagnosis. Discover how AI cancer care tools are reaching more oncologists and patients across the globe, so that one day the most remarkable thing about a cancer diagnosis is how unremarkable it becomes.

title: Tech Talks
description: Tech Talks explore the stories behind the most recognisable names in industry and the startups you’re yet to hear from. The innovation, cultural transformation and investment stories driving technology with host David Savage.

title: From prompt to product: Building products with AI [Hosted by Metalab]
description: ZERO, Metalab’s R&D team, breaks down how to use AI to design and build software, drawing from hands-on experience shipping real client work. Learn which tools matter and how to use them. Please be aware that attendance is first-come, first-served.

title: Inside Microsoft’s Vancouver dev hub [Hosted by Microsoft]
description: A panel of Microsoft Vancouver employees shares how multidisciplinary teams operate at scale and contribute to global products while maintaining a fast moving local culture.

title: Artificial intelligence for natural intelligence: Using AI to help people with dementia
description: Neuropsychological interventions can improve specific aspects of cognition, help people regain the ability to do what matters to them and slow the progression of symptoms. These treatments are supported by randomized clinical trials, but their labour-intensive nature has limited access for millions who could benefit. This talk explores how a new generation of AI is expanding access to these therapies and delivering meaningful improvements in people’s lives.

title: When AI sounds right but is wrong: The hidden crisis of context
description: As everyone races to deploy AI agents, one challenge continues to undermine real-world impact: context. Getting the right information to the model, reliably, securely, and accurately, is far harder than it looks. This is where most AI systems quietly fail. Discover why DIY approaches to context engineering often fall short, and what it takes to deliver enterprise-grade AI systems grounded in high-quality data, architected to minimize hallucinations at scale.

title: Opening remarks
description: Opening remarks with PJ Hagerty

title: The human-AI flywheel: Why your best people are your best AI strategy
description: The industry frames AI as a replacement story. The human-AI flywheel rejects that tradeoff, showing how human insight and AI execution create a feedback loop where automation quality improves continuously without sacrificing control. This talk reveals why the companies turning their operators into AI coaches and agent traffic controllers are outperforming pure automation plays on quality, speed and cost.

title: Why open source will win the agent era
description: Agents are expensive, complex, and long-running – making efficiency critical. Join Brightwave CEO Mike Conover as he explores why open source is structurally better suited to power them at scale and how it is redefining the economics of AI.

title: What a dumpling shop taught me about trust
description: Before Scale Social, Runbin ran a small dumpling business: no agency, no budget, no strategy worth putting on a slide. Then a customer shared a video that outperformed every ad he'd ever run. What followed was more than a marketing lesson. This talk traces that moment from a rented kitchen to the boardrooms of enterprise brands wrestling with the same problem at scale. In a world where brands can generate infinite content with AI, what's the one thing that actually can't be made?

title: Opening remarks

title: Solving drug discovery with AI: Stop searching and start creating

Explore how generative AI is shifting the pharmaceutical paradigm from a game of hide and seek to one of intentional design, creating entirely new, lab-ready molecules to treat the previously untreatable.

title: Consolidating the modern tech stack with AI
description: As tool sprawl hits its peak, companies are rethinking their tech stack. They are adopting fewer, smarter AI-driven systems that unify workflows, surface insights, and simplify decision making. Join this panel of experts as they explore how to consolidate your stack.

title: Safety first: Vibe coding at enterprise scale
description: For years, data-driven growth came with a catch: tool sprawl, a dedicated team and months of cleanup. Today, the fastest-growing teams are vibe coding their way to answers – empowering one person to do what once took an entire department. Discover the real lessons from this shift: what works, what breaks at scale and how to turn high-velocity workflows into durable, scalable systems without losing control.

title: Marketing the future before it exists
description: Tech wins when it feels inevitable, not just when it works. Fusion was “30 years away” for a century. Today, it is 24 months away. Learn the marketing blueprint for shifting a 100 year idea into an industrial milestone and turning scientific possibility into market certainty.

title: A business culture problem: Why your AI strategy is missing its foundation
description: Many companies focus on the flashy models and prompts of AI, but ignore what actually makes these systems work: the power, the infrastructure and the data foundations. Discover the roadmap to building a production-ready environment where AI agents can finally perform at their full potential.

title: How fandom is transforming entertainment
description: At the intersection of the creator economy and the entertainment industry, fandoms are reshaping which stories get told and distributed globally. Today’s streaming hits often begin with creators building loyal online communities, giving studios access to stories with prebuilt audiences. Join David J. Lee and Kelly Day for a deep dive into how fan-driven stories are scaled into streaming success.

title: Teaching machines the art of being human
description: A beautiful face on top of a robot is still a robot. Rendering alone cannot produce human connection. Real human computing requires personality, memory and judgement all working together so the machine doesn't just look like us, but actually understands us, remembers us and adapts to us over time. Explore what it takes to build full-stack AI humans that people genuinely trust and want to interact with again.

title: The Organization Man: Why scaling needs fewer managers
description: Companies today are slowed down by too many layers of managers and processes. Explore how AI is used to build a new era of the enterprise: one with fewer layers, less friction and more room for people to do great work.

description:
As competition reshapes and AI accelerates change, brands are being tested like never before. Lindsay Smith and Rene Thomas of Takt introduce the “Brand Matrix”, a framework to stress-test brand clarity and coherence.
Drawing on their work across global brands, they explore how companies can navigate shifting category boundaries, align on identity, and build brands that remain distinctive and adaptable.

title: Your graph is your moat: Knowledge and context are the big AI unlock
description: If 2025 marked the start of the agentic revolution, 2026 is shaping up to be all about context. Terms like context graphs, knowledge graphs, semantics, and ontologies are flooding the AI airwaves, with graphs as the unifying principle. Join Neo4j CTO Philip Rathle as he explores the why, what, and how of it all; breaking down how you can overcome your top AI obstacles and find a stable moat in this fast-evolving world.

This session explores the economic blueprint for second-life technology, showing how it delivers the profitable, domestic energy storage needed to break the power bottleneck for the AI and manufacturing sectors.

Sharing their feedback and expertise are IBM Ventures' Emily Fontaine, KPMG's Alexander Hanhan, MLSE's Jim O'Mara, and Innovate BC's Sheila Schindel.

Grounded in real creator experience and practical insight, they unpack the challenges behind creative work and discuss how platforms can become true champions of creators.

description: As businesses transition to the agentic enterprise, trust is becoming the most critical form of engineering. Join this session to explore how embedding ethical guardrails directly into the technology stack turns governance from a compliance checklist into a driver of performance.

title: What it actually takes to train frontier models
description:
Over the past year, the dominant AI conversation centered on the advancements of large language models. But a parallel shift is unfolding in generative visual models with its own distinct set of challenges. Text is rich in semantics but capped there. Pixels carry far more raw information, but extracting useful signals at scale is far less straightforward. 
In this fireside, Tim Dockhorn will unpack what it actually takes to train and deploy frontier multimodal systems in 2026. He’ll discuss how Blackwell-class hardware is reshaping model architecture and scaling decisions; why image generation remains far from a solved problem; and how visual pre-training is evolving into a foundation for more advanced capabilities, from video understanding to robotics and world models.

title: From a local first to global power: Scaling fusion
description: A world-first fusion machine is operating just 30 minutes from the Web Summit stage. Fusion is happening now, and Canada is a leader. This session explores how fusion will move from the lab to grid-ready power; the milestones, barriers, and scale-up principles that turn bold science into reliable energy for homes and industry.

title: Cracking the code: Neurodivergence and creative AI
description: As AI moves from concept to reality, the question is who is best equipped to navigate its complexity. Jen Friel, Founder of Talk Nerdy To Me, explores how neurodivergent thinkers bring a unique advantage in detecting patterns, anomalies, and blind spots in AI systems. She will reframe neurodiversity as a strategic advantage for better, more creative technology.

title: Is Open Source AI rewriting the rules of the tech industry?
description:
The technology sector is facing one of its most significant disruptions in decades, and the catalyst isn’t coming from a Silicon Valley boardroom - it’s coming from the open source community.
Join us for an exclusive press conference examining how the rapid proliferation of open source AI is upending the dominance of proprietary systems, challenging the business models of the world’s most powerful tech companies, and raising urgent questions about regulation, security, and the future of the industry. Who stands to win and lose as open models close the gap with closed ones? And what does this mean for Big Tech’s AI investments?

title: Canada’s AI Blind Spot: Why the Application Layer Matters
description: Canada is pouring resources into AI model training and infrastructure, but risks falling behind where today's battles are happening: at the application layer. As newer models quickly outpace costly in-house efforts, companies like Spellbook show that success lies in building on top of cutting-edge systems, not reinventing them. This talk argues for shifting resources from R&D to the application layer, where massive businesses are being created.

title: ILM: How visual effects builds new worlds
description: From Avatar: Fire and Ash to Sinners and the Jurassic World franchise, Industrial Light & Magic remains at the cutting edge of cinematic innovation. Join us for a behind-the-scenes look into 'Avatar: Fire and Ash' with Marko Chulev and Rachel Reed, where they share first-hand experiences of how the industry’s most ambitious worlds are imagined, built, and brought to life.

title: AI's survival of the fittest
description: The spray-and-pray approach to AI investing is dead. Join us as we discuss what it takes to stand out in a saturated market. We're moving beyond the hype to identify the startups building real moats, the innovative applications solving 'unsolvable' problems, and the specific traits that will define the big winners of the next 12 months.

title: Personal AI is here: Building intelligence that understands you
description: As AI moves beyond screens, always‑on devices like watches, earbuds, glasses, and new form factors are becoming the foundation for more contextual, continuous intelligence. These devices unlock new ways to sense intent, adapt to environments, and deliver experiences that feel truly personal. In this session, Alex shares what this shift means for the broader technology ecosystem, where new value will be created, and how intelligent experiences will scale across devices and platforms. The next era of AI will not be defined by a single product, but by connected systems designed around people.

speakers.name: Kedar Kondap
speakers.position: Senior Vice President and General Manager of Compute & Gaming

title: Every Customer, Every Question, Every Time: The Secret Behind 3-4x Conversion Improvements
description:
Your customers don't know if they're talking to sales or support, and they don't care. They have questions about specs, pricing, compatibility, delivery, returns. Companies that answer every question sell more, lose fewer customers, and earn more referrals.

Drawing on data from 200+ contact centers, Matt Price will show why AI's biggest commercial impact isn't cost reduction. It's the 3-4x conversion lift Crescendo customers see when service conversations become sales conversations.

The session closes with a live demo, a children's clothing retailer where a return becomes a multi-item order. The real question it raises: how much revenue have you been leaving on the table?

title: How AI is turning e-commerce from a storefront into an agent
description: Your next best customer might not be a person. It might be an agent. Shopping, comparing, buying on behalf of someone who never visits your site. Commerce was built for humans browsing. That era is ending. The next era belongs to the stores that can think, respond, and sell autonomously.

title: The parrot problem: How AI answers misrepresent your brand
description: Getting cited by AI is table stakes. The real challenge is shaping what models say about your brand once they do. In this session, learn how to keep AI-generated mentions accurate, current, and aligned with the story you want told. Because when AI gets it wrong, you lose customers before they ever reach your site.

title: Loneliness at the end of empire
description: In this conversation, Dr. Gabor Maté and Hasan Piker explore loneliness as a defining feature of life in the waning phase of Western hegemony. Tracing how trauma, disconnection and feelings of unfulfillment can be systemic, and not just personal. They discuss how a period marked by inequality, media saturation, tech hegemony, and political fragmentation, creates a sense of decline, which calls for new forms of connection and solidarity.

title: Energy at a Crossroads: Innovation in the Age of Crisis
description:
As geopolitical tensions reshape global energy supply chains and the demand for clean technology accelerates at an unprecedented pace, the energy sector finds itself at a defining moment.
This press conference brings together leading voices from across the clean energy industry to examine how organisations are responding to today’s most pressing challenges — and where the opportunities lie.            This press conference is held in the Media Village.
Media event only.

title: The AI-Native Happy Hour: Founders & Engineering Leaders by Scrut Automation
description: An exclusive, 90-minute networking reception for Founders and Engineering Leaders building AI-native products.  Join Scrut Automations VP Engineering, CISO, Nick Muy,  for an open bar, great food, and high-signal networking. Connect with fellow technical leaders to discuss scaling infrastructure, navigating compliance, and securing your stack without slowing down engineering velocity. No pitches, just peer-to-peer connections with the best. Please note space is limited, and this event is first come, first served.

title: Crafting a planetary-scale system that watches & forecasts the entire world
description: Every day, billions of stories unfold across the globe in thousands of languages told through video, audio, and text. See what it looks like to build a planetary-scale AI system that watches and reasons over the entire world in realtime, revealing the macro-level trends, uncovering hidden patterns, contextualizing, and even forecasting the future of where our world is heading.

title: Founder playbook: How to build an AI-native, product-first startup
description: As an engineer who spent years on the receiving end of enterprise software, Vineet learned what separates tools that get adopted from those that don’t. Now as a founder, he’s applied those hard-won lessons to building products in the age of AI that customers actually want to buy and use.

title: Fintech as a disrupter of traditional credit
description: This session explores how fintech is reshaping access to credit for underserved consumers. By using alternative data, flexible repayment models, and digital-first experiences, new lenders are challenging traditional credit systems expanding access while rethinking risk, pricing, and customer trust.

title: Shipping is easy. Landing is hard.
description: Every company is shipping more than ever. AI code delivery means more releases, more features, more changelogs competing for the same user attention. As ship velocity climbs, the user-product relationship becomes the scarce thing. Tomorrow's winners will treat the loop between user, ticket, and release as infrastructure, so every shipped feature reaches the users who've been waiting for it. Ben Vinegar  argues the next wave of startups won't win on ship velocity alone. They'll win on sticking the landing.

title: The state of (AI)nvesting
description: In a disciplined 2026 market, investors are obsessed with capital efficiency. AI-native startups are proving they can reach $10m+ in ARR with smaller and smaller teams. How do VCs see this market evolving, and what makes them remain ever-bullish on its potential for big returns?

title: Past the rulebook: where crypto actually gets built now
description: For years, crypto's defining tension was geographic: MiCA in Europe, licensing regimes across the Gulf and Asia, Canada's compliance-first model, and a fragmented US. That debate is moving on. Building is now happening across new categories: payments networks embedding digital assets, fintechs operating under clearer rules, and public-market balance sheets treating crypto as treasury infrastructure. A Canadian neobank, a global payments network, and an XRP treasury company unpack where crypto actually gets built now.

title: Inference infrastructure: Building efficient token factories
description: AI models are incredibly computationally intensive. Single tokens require 100s of billions of computations. Demand for inference is growing exponentially, and open source AI models are closing the gap. We explore what it takes to build efficient inference infrastructure, and how to optimize inference for agentic workloads.

title: Happy Hour [Hosted by Make]
description:
You started the conference day starving for knowledge. As it goes by, you become thirsty for a wind-down. Sounds familiar? Stop by Booth E355 and take a quick breather with beer, wine, and soda.

You'll see how Make's automation and AI agents turn complexity into a glass box where processes are visible, transparent, and logical. Come for the drinks, stay for the automation, leave with a solution.

title: Dynamic Women
description: Dynamic Women® How do I have more balance in my life? How do I have more time for everything that matters? Am I ready to make a big change? How do I make more money? How can I have more energy? And the most common question is, Do I really have what it takes to reach my goals? Whether you are a business owner, a leader, a professional, the “boss” of the home and kids, or looking to jump into your next chapter, this is the show for you.  The Dynamic Women® Podcast is an Award-Winning action-focused lifestyle and leadership podcast full of stories and strategies to help women design their success and unleash their “Dynamic Woman”. You can learn from the experts how to get clarity, build confidence, and get into action on your biggest goals and dreams.  Host Diane Rolston, a Professional Certified Coach (PCC) with over 20 years of experience teaching and training, shares with you her business strategies, leadership tools, lifestyle hacks, expert lessons, and motivational stories that will inspire you and support you to create a life and business you love! Diane is pulling back the curtain on what has helped her become an award-winning coach, speaker, and confident leader all while raising her kids. She breaks down processes she uses when coaching female leaders, so you can be dynamic in every area of life with more ease and confidence.  Not only will you get her tried and tested secrets, but she also interviews trailblazing experts who share stories of what helped them achieve success, as well as industry-specific tips you can apply to your life so you can achieve your success faster, easier, and with less effort.  The mission of Dynamic Women is to inspire more women to unleash their dynamic selves and boost their lives in all areas, especially business and leadership.

title: When software becomes a user
description: AI agents are logging into services, making decisions, and taking action across systems, often without direct human involvement. Join 1Password CTO Nancy Wang and Genspark co-founder & COO Wen Sang as they explore how this changes the rules entirely, because we’re no longer just managing people, we’re managing autonomous actors that we've created.

title: Why generic AI fails at the last mile
description: Most AI projects succeed in demos but break down in production. This keynote uses Manulife’s decision process to show why the “last mile” of AI - real data, real constraints, real risk - requires more than generic models. It explores why this gap is an infrastructure challenge, not a prompting one, and how enterprises transition to specialized AI without costly missteps.

title: Betting on a 'model-agnostic' future
description: In the race to dominate AI, most of the world is betting on who will build the smartest "brain". While the tech giants fight a multi-billion dollar war over LLM supremacy, value may be shifting from the "brain" to the "body," the orchestration layer that gets the work done. Join Gumloop CEO Max Brodeur-Urbas and Benchmark's Ev Randle as they discuss if the most defensible AI moat isn't a single model, but a model-agnostic platform.

description: Most technology today is built for people already connected – but billions still live beyond reliable internet access. Join this panel to explore how firsthand experiences working with farmers and Indigenous communities are shaping new approaches to digital tools, from AI at the edge to novel infrastructure. Black Eyed Peas artist and Filipino farmer advocate Apl.de.Ap joins a Canadian-born son of Vietnamese refugees to share lessons from the field on building technology designed for the world’s last-mile communities and the next billion users.

title: Web Summit After Hours: Tech Bros: are we the bad guys?
description: From startups to global platforms, the tech sector has reshaped how we work, communicate, and govern. But alongside innovation has come growing criticism. Increasing inequality, data power, market dominance and  excessive political influence. This talk takes a critical but balanced look at the role of “tech bros” in today’s political economy. Are they drivers of progress, or architects of new forms of power and control? Are they society's saviours or tomorrow’s antichrist? By unpacking the narratives around technology, capitalism, and responsibility, this talk asks a simple but uncomfortable question. Are we the bad guys?

Jennie Ito and Karla Martin unpack what this shift means for how we connect, create, and grow up online.

title: Engineering trust from the ground floor to outer space
description: What can space missions and construction sites teach us about building systems where failure has real world consequences? From spacecraft engineering to complex construction projects, high-stakes industries rely on precision, safety and trust. In this session, Manju Bangalore and Usman Shuja explore how these principles are evolving as AI becomes deeply integrated into engineering and design workflows.

title: Innovate BC Startup Showcase
description: Join us for a showcase of some of the most exciting startups currently operating in British Columbia!

title: Opening remarks

title: What shapes Gen Z’s worldview?
description: A generation raised on feeds is now forming opinions, identities, and political views online. As traditional media becomes less influential, younger audiences are choosing content that feels more raw, real, and participatory. This session explores how platforms and creators influence how Gen Z understands the world – and what responsibility comes with that role. As the lines between content, community, and civics blur, what does it take to engage young audiences with intention, trust, and impact?

speakers.name: Brandon Feldman
speakers.position: Director of News, Civics & Podcast Partnerships

title: Crafting a story investors can't ignore
description: Turn your pitch into a story investors believe. Learn why most decks fail, what investors look for, and how to clearly show customer pain, proof, and a credible path to scale—without fluff.

Connect with fellow innovators, founders, and industry leaders to exchange ideas, explore collaboration opportunities, and learn more about how IBM Ventures supports startups in scaling and succeeding. Enjoy great conversations in a relaxed setting designed to spark meaningful connections. Please note space is limited, and this event is first come, first served.

title: Agritech investing: Risk, returns, and repeatable growth [Hosted by Vancouver, BC, Canada]
description: Investors break down how they evaluate agritech: Unit economics, risk, ROI, and what separates pilots from venture-scale companies.

title: Stop speculating, start simulating
description: Strategic decision-making is fundamentally arbitrary. Despite a $153 billion market research industry, major business choices rely on flawed data, hidden biases, and outdated surveys. This leaves boardrooms operating on a foundation that is more fiction than fact, making it impossible to truly quantify the cost of a wrong turn.

title: Shifting gears or staying in neutral?
description: Autonomous vehicles promise major benefits: 24/7 operation, fewer crashes, lower emissions, and relief for driver shortages. But they also risk displacing hundreds of thousands of trucking jobs and disrupting communities. More broadly, advances in AI could threaten many blue-collar livelihoods, creating a new low-skilled underclass. This raises a key question: should we allow free-market innovation to drive progress, or impose regulations and limits to protect workers and social stability?

description: In an era where attention is currency and outrage drives engagement, the line between truth and profit in media has never been more blurred. This talk explores how modern news organizations, social platforms, and independent creators navigate the tension between informing the public and sustaining a business model.

At the other: specialists winning through deep domain expertise and speed. In the age of AI, the generalist VC is under threat. So what does survival look like, and who gets squeezed out?

title: International law and disorder: The crisis of justice
description: The international legal system is in crisis. In an era of Trump, Palantir, genocide and the risk of nuclear war, the systems meant to keep power in check look increasingly shaky. Have we entered an age where, as Thucydides said, "the strong do as they wish, and the weak suffer as they must"? This session digs into what happens when accountability breaks down, and asks the uncomfortable question: if the system isn’t working, what comes next?

title: The new currency of entertainment
description: In 2026, the entertainment landscape has hit a tipping point: authenticity is the rarest and most valuable asset. As AI-generated content and “perfect” algorithms flood our feeds, reality TV has pivoted from being a “guilty pleasure” to the primary source of human truth.

title: Is your scaling strategy future-proof?
description: As AI reshapes everything from lead generation to customer success, the traditional methods are being disrupted. Cathy Gao and Tina Gouldbourne discuss how Sapphire-backed companies, and Blue J, are leveraging AI to scale smarter, not larger. Join this session for inside tips and learn how to move past the pitch deck, into the real world of scaling.

title: 🤳 Building on socials meetup

title: From blue chips to red politics? Tech's ideological shift
description: This session explores Silicon Valley's changing political identity. Once aligned with liberal, “blue chip” values, parts of the industry are shifting toward more conservative or populist positions. Featuring journalists Eoin Higgins and Dara Kerr, the conversation will also reflect on recent high-profile legal battles involving Elon Musk and Sam Altman, and what they reveal about power, governance, and accountability in tech. It examines what’s driving this shift, from platform influence and culture wars to regulation, and what it means for the future of technology and politics.

title: An AI cure for the insurance waiting room
description: As market confidence in AI’s role in healthcare matures, companies are moving beyond the hype to scaled implementation. In the health insurance space, AI is impacting speed, transparency, and cost stability. By removing manual administrative burdens, Angle Health is improving the healthcare experience for modern employers and patients.

title: The lifesaving use of technology
description: What happens when a sports field becomes a battlefield? In 2021, the Afghanistan Youth Women’s National Football Team was forced into hiding after the Taliban’s return to power. This session explores the practical and ethical role of technology in humanitarian crises, showing how connectivity can serve as a vital tool for survival when physical borders close.

The content creation landscape is undergoing a dramatic transformation. This press conference will explore the sector's defining challenges, bringing together executives and creators at the center of the story: from AI-generated media and the ownership battles it’s sparked, to the collapse of legacy monetization models, evolving audience expectations and the attention economy.   
This press conference is held in the Media Village.
Media event only.

title: Media in the age of skepticism
description: As audiences grow more questioning and selective about the information they consume, this talk explores how trust in media is being reshaped  through technological change. Focusing on the rise of AI and other emerging tools, it examines both the risks, such as misinformation at scale, and the opportunities to enhance transparency, verification, and accessibility in news.

title: Scale-up or burnout
description: Whether you are shipping high-performance AI hardware or building global employment infrastructure, the challenge is the same: the tactics that won Series A will fail at unicorn scale. This talk reveals the tools for making the ultimate leap, moving from founder-led hustle to an automated, system-led machine.

title: The New Media expansion

Join EA SPORTS to explore how advancements in real-world data, digital human technology, and player creation tools are enabling more authentic, lifelike, and expressive interactive experiences – and shaping more connected, immersive environments for sports fans.

title: Should we stop making podcasts?
description: Independent media, defined by podcasts and blogs, promised to usher in an era of critical thinking away from the constraints of corporate pressures. However, in an age of algorithmic tailoring, has this new form of media created a generation addicted to dopamine inducing, click-baity media content, intended, not to inform, but to disrupt?

title: Engineering a resilient food system
description: High-density data is moving agriculture from guesswork to precision execution by synthesising millions of daily data points into actionable field intelligence. Amie Thesingh shares how to transform on-farm information into a powerful engine for building a more resilient, profitable, and sustainable future for global food systems.

title: How chess saved my life
description: A turbulent childhood in a cult. Becoming a chess prodigy. Co-founding the game’s biggest platform. Danny Rensch’s story is anything but typical. Join this candid fireside chat as he reflects on how chess shaped his life and how Chess.com helped fuel the game’s global resurgence. Expect sharp insights on the headlines shaking the chess world, from one its best-known leaders.

title: ICE vs the US Constitution
description: In Donald Trump's first year of his second term as President, he has forced ICE to engage in increasingly extreme measures in an effort to reduce the number of immigrants residing in the US. However, these efforts have regularly broken the very laws the President is supposed to uphold. In this session we will hear from a man who helped train ICE agents, until he could no longer stand by an allow the egregious violations of the constitution to continue, about how ICE is breaking the law and what can be done about it.

title: Who builds the future of gaming?
description: The global games industry is entering a new phase, shaped by rising development costs, shifting platform power, and the growing importance of long-term player engagement. This panel brings together leaders from across the ecosystem to explore how these forces are reshaping how games are made, funded, and sustained – and what it takes to compete in an increasingly complex market.

description: Between global population growth and accelerating protein demand, there’s a supply gap looming. This conversation explores how cultivated meat innovation can go from the dinner plate to a pillar of global nutrition security, covering everything from regulatory frameworks to cultivated chicken as a tool for consumer choice.

title: Future of design in 2030
description: As technology accelerates, which design principles will endure? Join Ammunition's Robert Brunner as he explores what will define great design at the end of the decade.

title: Hard lessons in high growth
description: How can you tackle the creative pressures of AAA gaming to the rapid-fire scaling of digital marketplaces to the world of investing in healthtech and medical innovations? Two tech founders, share hard-earned lessons on maintaining culture, evolving as a founder, and leading through the complex transitions that define world-class organisations.

title: When players become the platform
description: Roblox has evolved into a creator-driven ecosystem where games, communities, and economies are built by its users at massive scale. Jerret West sits down to unpack what that shift means for discovery, safety, and the future of game design. The conversation also explores how gaming changes when players are no longer just consuming worlds, but actively building them.

title: Why your pitch deck won't grow corn
description: Most climate companies never make it off the pitch deck. Upside Robotics is already on commercial corn farms across North America, replacing mass fertiliser sprays with precise, root-level application. This session will explore the intricacies of building a climate company, featuring the founder who lived in a camper to build her tech and an investor who backed her.

In this session, visionary companies demonstrate how they are scaling the next generation of global systems, moving beyond traditional software to create a more resilient, decentralised, and automated world.

title: What happens when AI reads the fine print?
description: Much of the current payments industry was built on confusion – hidden fees, deferred interest, and fine print that profits from mistakes and misunderstandings. What happens to that model when there’s a PhD-level financial advisor in every pocket? From co-founding PayPal to building Affirm, Max Levchin can explore how advances in artificial intelligence are rewiring the payments industry and what it means for merchants, consumers, and the future of commerce.

title: AI, software or humans
description: Every founder hits a wall: not everything that looks scalable actually is. Our experts break down how companies can hit $100M+ by knowing exactly when to use AI, when to build software, and when only a human will do.

title: Playful intelligence: Reimagining generative behavior
description: Over the last decade, the games industry has prioritized content scale, while players seek more depth. Join Artifical Agency Co-founder & CEO Brian Tanner as he examines this misalignment and explores how generative agents can shift game design from scripted experiences to living systems that make gameplay feel personal, dynamic, and impossible to predict.

description: This session will delve into the critical intersection of private equity, Indigenous-led projects, and the rapidly evolving sectors of energy, technology, and sustainable development.

title: Building to last: Lessons from two decades bootstrapped and the AI bet that came next
description: In a world where "growth at all costs" is the default setting, Articulate is an outlier. They bootstrapped to early profitability and spent two decades building a category-defining unicorn before partnering with outside investors. It's a move that sounds impossible in the modern AI era, yet it allowed them to quietly become the intelligent learning layer for 100 of the Fortune 100. As AI rewrites the rules, how can category leaders not just defend their lead, but seize the AI moment to accelerate it? And why, after becoming the industry leader, a bootstrapped giant finally decided to bring in investors and how they knew they found the right ones? Join this session with Edwin and Tengbo for a candid conversation about the reality of the "long game."

title: Creators vs platforms: Who owns the player relationship?
description: Streamers are building bigger, more loyal audiences than ever – but they don’t own the platforms they rely on. So who really controls the relationship with players? This session will unpack the power dynamics shaping gaming right now – from revenue splits to audience loyalty – and explores where creators have the most leverage with the platforms they call home, the brands they collaborate with, and the communities they’ve built.

title: Why tech got agriculture backwards
description: People are looking above ground to optimise agriculture, yet nature's most sophisticated intelligence has been running beneath our feet for 500 million years. Founder of Miraterra, Karn Manhas, explains that AI's most consequential breakthrough is from reading and giving voice to what the land already knows, and that Canada's soil may be its greatest untapped source of sovereignty.

This session brings together the co-founder of BioWare (and founder of ThresholdImpact) with two of its former employees, who then went on to start their own studios, Phoenix Labs and Critical Path Games. They'll look back on the early days of BioWare, explore lessons learned from that experience, and discuss how it informed their subsequent successes in the worlds of gaming, angel investing, and leadership.
